prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `66ac4c3b`.
codex_response:
Rule: identify the two aligned sparse marker lines, take the source-colored shape on the edge side of the nearer marker, mirror it across the farther marker, and recolor the copied shape to the nearer marker’s color.

I implemented the task package in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_66ac4c3b/__init__.py), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_66ac4c3b/verifier.py#L4), and [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_66ac4c3b/generator.py#L44). I used the Opus hint as a starting point, but discarded the Sonnet details and documented the mismatch in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_66ac4c3b/notes.md). No new shared primitive was needed.

The generator stays close to the task family by keeping the 22x22 layout, using two parallel sparse marker lines six cells apart, generating an edge-adjacent source blob in the second marker’s color, then producing rotated/reflected variants so the family covers the observed orientations without changing the underlying structure.

Validation:
- Official verifier check passed: `3/3`.
- Shared build completed for `1000` examples.
- Generated-example verification passed: `1000/1000`.
- I also sanity-checked the generated previews in terminal form; the outputs match the intended “marker lines + mirrored recolor” family.
